title: balance() query counts notes with block_height=0 as confirmed when scan_tip >= min_confirmations
description: db.rs balance() SQL: WHERE n.block_height <= threshold_i64. Notes inserted before migration v4 have block_height DEFAULT 0. When scan_tip >= min_confirmations, threshold_i64 = scan_tip - min_confirmations >= 0, so block_height=0 satisfies block_height <= threshold and those notes appear as confirmed. This is the nie-br9a.3 issue that the code claims is being fixed. Checking the current code: the threshold logic was fixed (uses -1 when scan_tip < min_confirmations) but when scan_tip >= min_confirmations, threshold = scan_tip - min_confirmations which could be 0 for a fresh wallet with scan_tip == min_confirmations, making block_height=0 notes appear confirmed. The WHERE clause does not exclude note_diversifier IS NULL (undecrypted notes) or block_height=0 sentinel notes. Trigger: any unspent note with block_height=0 in the DB when scan_tip >= min_confirmations. Bad outcome: falsely confirmed balance reported.
